title: DPA: incident notice windows & cross-border forensic handoff (action items)
WaveHealth Dedicated capacity: scheduling deep-dive for throughput/latency
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex@wavehealth.ai>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@wavehealth.ai>
Date: Sun, 20 Dec 2026 14:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood Dedicated — next steps

Hi Alex — thanks for signing up and for the details in the intake form. I’m Ben on the Redwood sales team.

We’d love to set up a 45-minute walkthrough focused on Dedicated capacity and how we’d support your latency and throughput SLOs. Based on what you shared, the session would cover:
- workload profile (QPS, token lengths)
- typical latency targets and tail behavior
- autoscale/overflow policies and fallbacks
- expected unit economics and cost model

Proposed 45m windows (PT):
- Tue 2026-12-22 10:00–10:45 
- Tue 2026-12-22 14:00–14:45 
- Wed 2026-12-23 09:30–10:15

If none of those work, paste 2–3 times that do and I’ll coordinate. I’ve also included a short intake worksheet (wavehealth_intake_sheet.xlsx) to capture any additional perf constraints ahead of the call.

Calendly if you'd rather pick: https://calendly.com/redwood-inference/dedicated-demo-45m

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachment: wavehealth_intake_sheet.xlsx


From: Alex Turner <alex@wavehealth.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@wavehealth.ai>
Date: Sun, 20 Dec 2026 17:10:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood Dedicated — next steps

Thanks Ben — appreciate the quick reply.

A few notes:
- We’re EST (UTC-5). The best window for us is afternoon EST.
- Rough workload: baseline 60–100 qps, occasional spikes to ~180 qps for 30–60s. Typical prompt size is 800–2,000 tokens (system + user + history).
- Latency target: p95 <= 250ms for standard generation (non-streamed) and stable p99 behavior is important for SLA.

Can you do Tue 2026-12-22 at 14:00 PT (which should be 17:00 EST)? Also please send any pre-read on how you measure p95/p99 and sample benchmark artifacts. We’ll likely have our infra lead (Sam) on the call.

—Alex
Alex Turner
Head of Data Products | WaveHealth
alex@wavehealth.ai | (212) 555-8843


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex@wavehealth.ai>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@wavehealth.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 20 Dec 2026 17:34:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Interested in Redwood Dedicated — next steps

Perfect — Tue 2026-12-22 14:00 PT / 17:00 EST works. I’m looping in Marissa (solutions engineering) for the technical deep-dive. She’ll run through our throughput tests, KV/prefix caching behavior, and quantization impact on latency.

Marissa — quick context: WaveHealth (Alex + Sam) is targeting sustained 60–100 qps with short spike capacity to ~180 qps. Typical context lengths up to ~2k tokens. Alex called out p95 <= 250ms and stable p99 as critical. Attached is the intake worksheet Alex filled; Marissa, can you confirm any additional artefacts you’d like before the call?

I’ll send the calendar invite once we confirm.

Thanks,
Ben

> 
> A few notes:
> - We’re EST (UTC-5). The best window for us is afternoon EST.

> - Latency target: p95 <= 250ms for standard generation (non-streamed) and stable p99 behavior is important for SLA.
> 
> Can you do Tue 2026-12-22 at 14:00 PT (which should be 17:00 EST)? Also please send any pre-read on how you measure p95/p99 and sample benchmark artifacts. We’ll likely have our infra lead (Sam) on the call.
> 
> —Alex


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex@wavehealth.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Sam Patel <sam.patel@wavehealth.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 Dec 2026 09:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: WaveHealth Dedicated capacity: scheduling deep-dive for throughput/latency

Thanks for the context — I’ll run the technical portion. A couple quick questions so we can prepare targeted artifacts: 

1) Are the spikes (to ~180 qps) bursty (seconds) or sustained but short (30–60s)? That affects cache/queue design.
2) What percentage of requests are single-turn vs multi-turn (i.e., how often do you include history)?
3) Any data residency or private deployment constraints (VPC/on-prem) we should know before discussing routing/fallbacks?

Pre-reads we can share before the call:
- short note on measuring p95/p99 and how continuous batching affects tail latency
- sample benchmark runs for 1k/2k token contexts (PDF) — attached (sample_benchmark_requirements.pdf)
- a short checklist for what we’ll need from your infra team (keys, firewalls, test harness)

Proposed refined windows (PT) — I have both slots open and can lead the deep technical segment in either: 
- Tue 2026-12-22 14:00–14:45 PT (confirmed by Ben)
- Wed 2026-12-23 15:00–15:45 PT (alternate)

If Tue 14:00 PT still works for you (Alex), Ben will send the invite. I’ll also add a 15-minute pre-call buffer for quick infra checks if Sam wants to join early.

Regards,
Marissa Cole
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

Attachment: sample_benchmark_requirements.pdf


From: Alex Turner <alex@wavehealth.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@wavehealth.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 Dec 2026 19:12:00 -0500
Subject: Re: WaveHealth Dedicated capacity: scheduling deep-dive for throughput/latency

Thanks — Tue 2026-12-22 14:00 PT / 17:00 EST confirmed for Alex + Sam. We appreciate the pre-read PDF and the intake sheet — I’ll have Sam check the checklist items.

A couple of asks before the call:
- Please include a brief (1–2 slide) summary of how Redwood enforces p95/p99 SLOs operationally (measures, alerts, fallbacks).
- If you can share a short command or script we can run locally to generate a representative 2k-token synthetic workload (just to sanity-check our harness pre-demo), that would be great.

Ben — please send the calendar invite and invite link. Also please confirm whether the session will be recorded and if you’ll share the recording after.

See you Tuesday — happy holidays.

—Alex

title: Security intake — SOC 2 / DPA / pen-test scheduling and evidence flow
preview:
From: Amy Tan <amy.tan@clearnova.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-12-12T09:14:00-05:00
Subject: Security intake — SOC 2 / DPA / Pen test kickoff

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick intro earlier. As part of our procurement checklist at ClearNova we need to run an intake that covers: SOC 2 evidence mapping, DPA confirmation for data flows, and a pen-test scoping session so our third-party vendor can size the engagement. Ideally we'd do a single intake to keep context, estimate 60–75 minutes.

Preferred windows: Dec 20–22 (AM CET) or Dec 21 (US afternoon). Can your team propose 2–3 slots and any pre-reads you need?

I've attached our current asset inventory and questionnaire to accelerate the triage (security-asset-list.xlsx).

Thanks,
Amy Tan
Security Ops Manager
ClearNova Payments
Attachments: security-asset-list.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Amy Tan <amy.tan@clearnova.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-12-12T11:27:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Security intake — SOC 2 / DPA / Pen test kickoff

Hi Amy — great to hear from you and thanks for the asset list. Looping in Rachel (Customer Success) and Marcus (SE) so we can staff the right folks.

We can do a combined session but from experience it helps to split the session into two focused segments (30 min pen-test scoping + 30 min DPA/SOC 2 evidence map) so we can keep each discussion actionable. Proposed slots (all shown in CET / convert as needed):

- Mon Dec 20 — 09:00 / 09:30 CET
- Tue Dec 21 — 13:00 / 13:30 CET
- Wed Dec 22 — 08:30 / 09:15 CET

If any of those work, we can send calendar invites and a quick pre-read. I've uploaded our intake template and a short pre-read outline to the shared drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2B_redwood-intake (redwood-intake-template.pdf attached).

Notes:
- For the pen-test scoping we'll request a brief inventory and network diagram up front.
- For evidence transfer we prefer DocuSign/secure link + short-lived access to a bucket; we can send an NDA if needed.

Attach: redwood-intake-template.pdf

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference.com
Attachments: redwood-intake-template.pdf

From: Amy Tan <amy.tan@clearnova.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-12-13T08:04:00+01:00
Subject: Re: Security intake — SOC 2 / DPA / Pen test kickoff

Thanks Ben — Rachel/Marcus, thanks for joining the thread.

We'd like to take Tue Dec 21 at 13:00 CET, but one of our ops leads just moved a dependent meeting up 1 hour. Is there any possibility to shift to 12:00 CET on the 21st? If not, we can keep 13:00 but wanted to check first.

On Sat, Dec 12, 2026 at 11:27 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com> wrote:
> Hi Amy — great to hear from you and thanks for the asset list. Looping in Rachel (Customer Success) and Marcus (SE) so we can staff the right folks.
>
> We can do a combined session but from experience it helps to split the session into two focused segments (30 min pen-test scoping + 30 min DPA/SOC 2 evidence map) so we can keep each discussion actionable. Proposed slots ...

Regards,
Amy
Security Ops Manager
ClearNova Payments

From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.inference.com>
To: Amy Tan <amy.tan@clearnova.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-12-14T09:22:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Security intake — SOC 2 / DPA / Pen test kickoff

Hi all — quick internal note from our side: our security engineer (Luis) is available on Dec 21 but prefers the earlier slot. We can do a two‑part format:

1) 12:00–12:30 CET — Pen test scoping (high-level attack surface, vendor expectations)
2) 12:40–13:10 CET — DPA / SOC 2 evidence map + evidence handoff process

If you can confirm we will:
- Send two calendar events with the internal dial-in and attendee list
- Share a short pre-read with the items needed from ClearNova (pre-read-checklist.md)

Happy to compress or expand based on your team's needs.

Best,
Rachel Kim
Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
Attachments: pre-read-checklist.md

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Amy Tan <amy.tan@clearnova.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.inference.com>
Date: 2026-12-20T16:02:00-08:00
Subject: Confirmed: Dec 21 — security intake (split sessions)

Hi Amy — thanks for being flexible. Confirming the schedule per Rachel's suggestion: two short sessions on Tue Dec 21 (times below are CET / your calendar should show converted times):

- 12:00–12:30 CET — Pen test scoping (Luis, Marcus)
- 12:40–13:10 CET — DPA / SOC 2 evidence map (Rachel, Ben)

Calendar invites have been sent (Google Calendar link: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=redwood-clearnova-intake). For secure evidence exchange, please sign the short NDA and upload artifacts to the secure folder we will provision via DocuSign link: https://www.docusign.com/na/documents/abcdef123456. HubSpot activity recorded: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/deal/CLN-ENTERPRISE-2026-0927 (activity id: 78234).

Attachments included for your reference: redwood-intake-template.pdf, pre-read-checklist.md, and a zipped starter evidence package (redwood-evidence-package.zip) with examples of the filenames we request.

If anything changes we'll update the invites — otherwise we look forward to the call.

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.inference.com
Bcc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.inference.com>
Attachments: redwood-intake-template.pdf, pre-read-checklist.md, redwood-evidence-package.zip

Subject: Change order: credits repackage + SOW recast

Hi Sanjay / procurement team,

Thanks again for the call this morning. Per our discussion, attached is a draft change order that: 
- Repackages part of the committed GPU capacity into a pool of burst credits (to smooth utilization spikes),
- Recasts Exhibit A (Implementation SOW) into two phases so Milestone 1 can be delivered earlier, and
- Applies a modest one-time credit to offset the implementation shift (see section 2.3).

What we need from you to move to signature:
1) Confirmation that the burst credits concept is acceptable (we've included a 90-day expiry on credits in the draft).
2) Clarify whether Procurement requires any alternate billing codes for the reclassified line items.

preview:
From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@heliosanalytics.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.example>
Cc: Priya Sharma <priya.sharma@heliosanalytics.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: customer quote + logo permission for case study

Hi Mateo —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our marketing team would like to run a short case-study blurb and include the Redwood logo on a landing page. Two quick asks from our side: (1) can we use a 1–2 sentence quote attributed to Helios Analytics, and (2) permission to display Redwood's logo alongside Helios on the microsite?

We will follow any embargo window you require and can accept limited wording. I attached a draft testimonial the team put together.

Best,
Jordan
Senior Product Ops
Helios Analytics

Attachment: Helios_testimonial_draft.docx


From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.example>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@heliosanalytics.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.example>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.example>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: customer quote + logo permission for case study

Jordan —

Appreciate the draft. Two internal items we need to confirm before signoff:

1) Publicity clause in our MSA/order form — our standard language limits reference rights to a short customer quote and logo use only if the customer: (a) confirms the final quote in writing, (b) agrees to an approved embargo date or immediate publish, and (c) grants perpetual, worldwide, royalty-free rights for Redwood to use the quote/logo in marketing materials.
2) We have a privacy gating requirement: testimonials may not include Helios customer data or screenshots containing PII without a separate asset approval.

Kimberly/Hannah — can legal confirm whether the attached MSA_redlines_v2.pdf (I pulled the latest redlines from procurement) are sufficient to cover the proposed quote + logo use? Preferred outcomes: explicit line in the order form that: (i) authorizes the exact quote text, (ii) requires final approval via reply-all, and (iii) limits logo files to an approved set. If we need more restrictive language (time-limited license or embargo windows), call that out.

Jordan — I cc'd our legal and contracts leads so we can close quickly. If you can confirm that the quote in the docx is the final verbiage, that speeds this up.

Thanks,
Mateo
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: MSA_redlines_v2.pdf, Helios_testimonial_draft.docx


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.example>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.example>, Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@heliosanalytics.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.example>
Date: Mon, 14 Dec 2026 10:25:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: customer quote + logo permission for case study

Hi all —

Quick legal read: the MSA_redlines_v2.pdf covers the basics but I recommend three specific edits before we publish anything:

- Publicity/Reference Rights: change from blanket "perpetual, worldwide" to "perpetual for marketing use as defined in Section 2.3, subject to Customer's final written approval of the quote and a logoset approval process." This clarifies we won't reuse disallowed logo variants.
- Embargo: add an optional embargo field on the order form (Order Form: Section 1.5) so customers can specify a release date; default to immediate if blank.
- Asset Approval: require that any screenshots, dashboards, or excerpts that contain derived metrics be redacted or submitted for separate asset approval.

If Helios confirms the quote text and signs the short addendum in the order form, I can approve the testimonial and logo usage. I attached a one-page snippet you can paste into the order form to capture these items (I included bracketed language and a short signature line).

Jordan — if you want us to keep the rights narrower (time-limited logo license or limited channels), let us know now. Otherwise the language below is what we typically push to marketing.

Suggested order-form snippet (paste-ready):
"[Publicity & Reference Rights] Customer grants Redwood a non-exclusive, royalty-free right to use the Final Approved Quote and Customer's official logo in Redwood marketing materials worldwide. Customer must provide final written approval of the Quote and an official logo file prior to publication. Customer may specify an embargo date in the Order Form; absent an embargo, publication may occur upon written approval. Any assets containing customer data or non-public metrics require separate asset approval."

-Kim
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

Attachment: orderform_publicity_snippet.txt


From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@heliosanalytics.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo.alvarez@redwood.example>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.example>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.example>
Date: Mon, 20 Dec 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: customer quote + logo permission for case study

Thanks everyone — this looks good. I confirmed with Priya (marketing) and we accept the suggested snippet, embargo set to 2027-01-15 for the product launch, and we will provide the official logo files in SVG/PNG. We also confirm no screenshots with live customer data will be used.

Priya and I have signed the order form addendum via DocuSign, and I uploaded the signed stub here: https://redwood.app/docusign/DW-4931

Mateo — please proceed to publish the approved quote after the embargo date and notify me of the permalink.

Jordan

Helios Analytics

title: [Polaris Analytics] Model upgrade caused latency spike & accuracy regressions
preview:
From: Elena Marquez <elena@polarisanalytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 19 Dec 2026 17:42:00 -0800
Subject: Urgent: large p99 spike + hallucinations since last night's model upgrade

Hi Ben / Marissa,

We pushed to prod around 02:20 UTC last night (your side told us model redwood-llm-v2.1 / quant profile int8-qp-1 was live) and since then we've seen: 
- p95 jumping from ~120ms to ~420ms; p99 to ~1.1s.
- 5xx error rate increased from ~0.7% to ~4.2% over the last 12 hours.
- A measurable uptick in incorrect entity linking / hallucinations in samples from our fraud-detection assistant (samples attached).

This is customer-facing and affecting our risk scoring pipelines. We've opened a ticket on our side but need immediate coordination. Can we get an incident bridge + a rollback plan?

I've attached a small bundle: p99_traces.csv (traces over the last 8 hours) and error-samples.jsonl (representative prompts + outputs).

Please advise on next steps — we need a mitigation window within the next 2 hours.

Thanks,
Elena
Lead Engineer, Polaris Analytics
Elena.Marquez@polarisanalytics.com

[attachment: p99_traces.csv (csv)]
[attachment: error-samples.jsonl (jsonl)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Marquez <elena@polarisanalytics.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Dec 2026 18:01:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Urgent: large p99 spike + hallucinations since last night's model upgrade

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Elena Marquez <elena@polarisanalytics.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Dec 2026 17:42:00 -0800
Subject: Urgent: large p99 spike + hallucinations since last night's model upgrade
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>

Team — forwarding an urgent customer report. They point at the redwood-llm-v2.1 + int8-qp-1 deployment from last night.

Marissa / Vivek / Lauren: can you triage? I think this needs an immediate mitigation (rollback or traffic split) and a customer-facing exec update. I will coordinate the bridge and invite Polaris leadership (Adam) if you want exec-level signoff for a rollback or credit.

Pls confirm receipt and an ETA for first action.

Ben
Account Exec, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

[Forwarded attachments: p99_traces.csv, error-samples.jsonl]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Marquez <elena@polarisanalytics.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Dec 2026 19:12:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Urgent: large p99 spike + hallucinations since last night's model upgrade

Quick triage notes (first 30m):
- We've reproduced the latency and error signals in our prod telemetry for Polaris' tenant. p99 for their hot path increased ~3x (180ms -> 520ms) starting at 02:17 UTC. Error rate (502/504) up ~3.5%.
- Correlated with a quant-profile switch event: int8-qp-1 was rolled to their dedicated pool as part of last night's optimization rollout. Kernel durations for the attention ops increased on A100 nodes for longer sequences; decode stalls observed.
- Also seeing a small but real regression in top-1 accuracy for key prompts used by their fraud assistant — outputs are more likely to drop entity context. Roughly +8% incorrect entity extractions in our sample set.

Attachments: quant-profile-diff.yaml (diff between currently live qp and previous qp-0.9), p99_traces.csv (extracted from their tenant ID).

Immediate mitigations I recommend: 
1) Traffic split to v2.0 non-quant variant for the hot path (can be done instantly with routing policy).
2) If customer agrees, rollback their dedicated pool to qp-0.9 (revert quant profile). That has the highest chance of fixing both latency & accuracy regressions.
3) Short-term scale-up: add 2 capacity nodes to their dedicated pool to reduce queueing while we test the above.

If we do steps 1+2 we should see p99 drop within a single quant-window (~10-15 minutes).

title: Capacity strain impacting VortexPay reserved pool — immediate mitigation & exec handoff
preview:
From: Elena Soto <elena@vortexpay.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Dec 2026 16:42:00 -0800
Subject: Spikes in latency + 5xx errors on reserved pool (VortexPay)

Alex —

We started seeing elevated tail latency and a rising 5xx rate on our production endpoints served from the reserved pool around 15:10 PST. Over the last two hours:

- P95 latency jumped from ~120ms to 420–700ms for several routes.
- 5xx rate climbed to ~4.8% (normally <0.3%).
- Throughput appears limited: token throughput dropped by ~22% vs baseline.

I've attached a short CSV with request samples and a small trace. We have a customer call Monday and this is blocking critical flows — requesting immediate help.

We are on the plan D-9134 (reserved set), so this is hitting production SLA.

Can someone from Redwood ops triage urgently?

Thanks,
Elena
Lead SRE, VortexPay

Immediate asks:
- Karthik / Marcus: can you confirm whether their dedicated pool is saturated? Check queue depth, GPU memory pressure, and any recent config rollouts in the last 24h.
- Naomi / Marissa: start comms with the customer (calm, concrete), and prepare a short list of remediation options and likely timelines. We should be ready to offer short-term credits if this materially impacts their SLAs.
- I'll open a troubleshooting call at 11:00 PT tomorrow (20 Dec). Please be ready.

Attaching current topology snapshot and Elena's CSV.

Alex
Senior Solutions Eng, Redwood Inference

On Sat, 19 Dec 2026 at 16:42 Elena Soto <elena@vortexpay.com> wrote:
> Alex —
> 
> We started seeing elevated tail latency...


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Elena Soto <elena@vortexpay.com>
Date: Sat, 19 Dec 2026 20:40:00 -08:00
Subject: RE: Spikes in latency + 5xx errors on reserved pool (VortexPay)

Short triage summary (initial pass):

- Observed: Their reserved pool is at ~98% GPU util on average across nodes for the past 90m; queue depth increased sharply at 15:05 PST. Several nodes show OOM-triggered micro-restarts and repeated KV cache evictions.
- Likely cause: A canary rollout from yesterday changed the batching policy for a high-volume route (batch_max_tokens increased + adaptive batching disabled). That change increased per-request memory footprint and reduced effective throughput under mixed seq len.
- Ancillary: model variant pinned in their reservation is the full fp16 variant (higher mem) rather than our quantized fallback; routing falls back poorly when nodes are saturated.

Immediate mitigations I recommend (ranked):
1) Apply hotfix: revert the canary batching change for the affected route (5–10m).
2) Rapidly add 4 burst-standby nodes to the customer's dedicated pool (30–45m to be ready).
3) Enable automatic fallback to quantized-compatible variant for their reservation (config flip, 10m).
4) Temporarily restrict prefix cache size to reduce eviction storms (5m).

Operational notes: I'll open JIRA RE-SERV-7812, and push metrics to the incident channel. Marcus, can you approve/trigger the burst node allocation? We will need priority scheduling from infra.

Karthik
Head of Inference Infrastructure

> On Sat, 19 Dec 2026 at 17:00 Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Team — escalating. VortexPay...

- If SLA credit appropriate: Sales & Finance will discuss scope on the bridge and follow up with formal credit terms.

title: Proposal: availability guarantees + tiered retainer scaffold
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Logan Pierce <logan@compasscloud.com>, Emma Roberts <emma@compasscloud.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Proposal: availability guarantees + tiered retainer scaffold

Hi Logan / Emma,

Thanks again for the demo last week — great discussion on predictable latency and the multi-region failover requirement. Attached is a first-pass scaffold that maps (1) uptime bands, (2) response SLAs, (3) retainer structures, and (4) discount tiers tied to annual commits. The goal here is to make the trade-offs explicit so procurement can sign off quickly.

Highlights: 
- 99.9% / 99.95% / 99.99% uptime bands with explicit credit slabs.
- Response windows (P1/P2/P3) and what’s included in each retainer level.
- A two-year discount ladder for 12/24/36 month commits and an onboarding credit for the first month.

Ask: Can you confirm which uptime band CompassCloud needs for production (we surfaced 99.95% as the working target)? Also, please flag any procurement terms we should pre-accept (term length, auto-renew, PO vs invoice).

Attached: guarantee_scaffold_v1.pdf (high-level) — I’ll follow with a detailed pricing matrix.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference


From: Logan Pierce <logan@compasscloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Emma Roberts <emma@compasscloud.com>
Date: Fri, 11 Dec 2026 10:50:00 -0800

From: James Park <james.park@catalystx.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 10 Dec 2026 14:03:00 -0800
Subject: Scaling pilot + pricing request (CatalystX)

Ben —

Appreciate the demo last week. CatalystX is greenlighting a rapid expansion of the pilot: we expect to move from ~50k tokens/day to ~1.2M tokens/day over the next 8 weeks if performance holds. Our legal + procurement asked for a clear commercial proposal to lock in capacity and enable an accelerated onboarding (we need dedicated routing and a predictable rate for our clinical inference workloads).

Two asks we need for an internal go/no-go next Tuesday:
- A one-time onboarding discount of 30% on billed tokens for months 1–3 to offset migration costs.
- A modified packaging: allow us to pre-buy a 9-month commitment at a blended effective rate (with a floor) rather than the standard tier.

We can commit to a minimum of $180k ARR if terms are reasonable; larger expansion is likely across their US clinics in H2. Can you confirm feasibility and send a term sheet? I attached our draft MAF (CatalystX_MAPlan_Q4.xlsx) and the org wants a marked term sheet back by Friday so procurement can begin redlines.

Thanks — James
James Park
VP Product, CatalystX
james.park@catalystx.com
+1 415-555-0142

Attachments: CatalystX_MAPlan_Q4.xlsx


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Scaling pilot + pricing request (CatalystX) — request for VP/CFO clearance

Team — forwarding CatalystX ask. Summary and recommended path below. Please treat as time-sensitive (customer wants a reply by Friday).

Quick summary: CatalystX wants a rapid ramp (50k -> 1.2M tok/day) and requests a one-time 30% onboarding discount for months 1–3 + a 9-month blended commitment with an effective floor. They indicate ~$180k ARR minimum if we greenlight.

Why I recommend exception: strategic vertical fit (clinical inference), large expansion runway, and they’ll sign a 9-month prepay/commit which reduces churn risk. We have similar precedent but not at this scale or compression.

Recommended approval (what I’m asking you to sign off):
- One-time onboarding credit equal to 30% of billed token charges for months 1–3 (applied as a lump credit at month 1 invoice).
- Allow 9-month blended commitment structure with a guaranteed monthly minimum equal to 40% of the committed ARR for the first 3 months, then ramp up.
- Require dedicated routing to isolated pool (Dedicated) to preserve latency/SLO; pass-through cost uplift for dedicated pool to customer (we can cap at +8% surcharge).

Financial impact (preliminary):
- Upside: $1.08M ARR over 9 months at expected volume if they scale as projected.
- Downside (cost delta for 30% credit + dedicated uplift): see attached cost_impact_30pct.csv. Ballpark gross margin delta in Q1 ~ -6 percentage points versus standard pricing but recoverable by month 6.

Attachments: term sheet draft (term_sheet_redwood_v2.pdf), cost impact csv.

Ask: Marcus/Laura/Marissa — please review and advise ASAP. If we proceed, we need VP/CFO written clearance to move forward and send the marked term sheet back to James.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
(650) 555-0199

Quick summary: CatalystX wants a rapid ramp (50k -> 1.2M tok/day) and requests a one-time 30% onboarding discount for months 1–3 + a 9-month blended commitment with an effective floor. They indicate ~$180k ARR minimum if we greenlight.

Why I recommend exception: strategic vertical fit (clinical inference), large expansion runway, and they’ll sign a 9-month prepay/commit which reduces churn risk. We have similar precedent but not at this scale or compression.

Recommended approval (what I’m asking you to sign off):
- One-time onboarding credit equal to 30% of billed token charges for months 1–3 (applied as a lump credit at month 1 invoice).
- Allow 9-month blended commitment structure with a guaranteed monthly minimum equal to 40% of the committed ARR for the first 3 months, then ramp up.
- Require dedicated routing to isolated pool (Dedicated) to preserve latency/SLO; pass-through cost uplift for dedicated pool to customer (we can cap at +8% surcharge).

Financial impact (preliminary):
- Upside: $1.08M ARR over 9 months at expected volume if they scale as projected.
- Downside (cost delta for 30% credit + dedicated uplift): see attached cost_impact_30pct.csv. Ballpark gross margin delta in Q1 ~ -6 percentage points versus standard pricing but recoverable by month 6.

Attachments: term sheet draft (term_sheet_redwood_v2.pdf), cost impact csv.

Ask: Marcus/Laura/Marissa — please review and advise ASAP. If we proceed, we need VP/CFO written clearance to move forward and send the marked term sheet back to James.

Thanks,
Ben Carter

title: Post-call: on-prem inference requirements, network boundaries & next owners
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@therahealth.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 14:22:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call: on-prem inference requirements, network boundaries & next owners

Thanks for the time today — appreciative of the clear constraints you shared. Below is a concise recap from our call (items I heard and what we propose next). Please flag anything I misunderstood.

Summary of key pain points / constraints
- Air-gapped processing requirement for PHI-derived models (no outbound internet from the inference cluster).
- Data residency: EU-only storage and audit logs; backups must remain within their EEA region.
- Procurement: hardware will be procured by end of Q1; want a slim SOW and deployment checklist to attach to the tender.
- Network: strict egress control, allowlist-based connectivity for updates, and a narrowly scoped ops tunnel for monitoring (mutually agreed IPs only).

Goals we discussed
- Proof of concept: isolated on-prem inference cluster in their Frankfurt DC running a 2-node Redwood runtime by mid-Q2.
- Ops model: customer-managed network and key management (bring-your-own-KMS).
- Security evidence: provide SOC 2 self-attestation, deployment hardening guide, and sample audit logs.

Immediate next steps (owners & ETA)
1) Camila: share a tailored mutual action plan + a sanitized network template for the tender (attachment 1 & 2) — by 2026-12-17.
2) TheraHealth: circulate internal network diagram and list of security controls required (Elena/Tom) — by 2026-12-22.
3) Ben: assemble an internal ops + eng readiness note with deployment checklist and estimate for the POC (Ben/Monica) — by 2026-12-23.
4) All: schedule a technical deep-dive (1 hour) week of 2027-01-11 to confirm allowlist/IPs and the monitoring tunnel approach.

Attachments: redwood-postcall-map.pdf (high-level MAP), thera_network_template.xlsx (network template for tender).

Quick ask: can you confirm whether the air-gap allows for an outbound management tunnel to a specific IP range (we proposed a 2-way SSH tunnel limited to 10.10.0.0/24 earlier), or if it must be strictly one-way (inbound only)? That detail affects how we design the monitoring fallback.

Thanks — looking forward to the next steps.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0123

---
Attachments referenced: redwood-postcall-map.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB), thera_network_template.xlsx (Excel, 96KB)

From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@therahealth.io>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Gallagher <tom.gallagher@therahealth.io>
Date: Wed, 16 Dec 2026 09:10:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Post-call: on-prem inference requirements, network boundaries & next owners

Thanks Camila — this aligns well with our notes. A few clarifications from our side:

1) Air-gap nuance: we can allow an outbound, customer-initiated management tunnel to a short list of IPs (egress-only from their network to an allowlisted ops IP). Inbound access to the cluster from the internet is not permitted.
2) KMS: we will absolutely use our HSM-backed KMS; Redwood will need to support customer KMS endpoints only accessible from within our network.
3) Procurement timeline: hardware RFQ goes live 01-Feb-2027; our legal has requested a two-page high-level SOW and an example security appendix for the RFQ package by 20-Jan-2027.

Can you include a quick one-paragraph description of how Redwood handles emergency patching for on-prem nodes that must not reach the internet? That will help our security and procurement teams too.

I'll share our network diagram and a short list of required controls by 2026-12-22 as discussed.

Best,
Elena Ruiz
Head of Infrastructure | TheraHealth
elena.ruiz@therahealth.io

On Tue, Dec 15, 2026 at 2:22 PM Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai> wrote:
> [see original message]

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 17 Dec 2026 08:05:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Post-call: on-prem inference requirements, network boundaries & next owners

Forwarding Elena's confirmation — please see her clarifications inline. Key asks for product/ops:
- Support for customer-managed HSM/KMS endpoints reachable only from internal network (no public KMS endpoints).
- Egress-only management tunnel (customer-initiated); we need to validate our agent approach supports that mode.
- Provide an emergency patching workflow that works without general internet access — likely via signed artifacts pushed through the allowlist.

Can you pull together a short internal note (deployment-checklist.md attached) with POC sizing and a bulletized emergency patch workflow we can hand to procurement? Also include any risks if the customer insists on no outbound management tunnel (i.e., fully isolated air-gap).

I'll draft the two-page high-level SOW and security appendix for the RFQ; Ben, can you review the SOW draft the week of 2026-12-20?

Thanks,
Camila

---

From: Elena Ruiz <elena.ruiz@therahealth.io>

Subject: Re: Post-call: on-prem inference requirements, network boundaries & next owners
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Gallagher <tom.gallagher@therahealth.io>

> Thanks Camila — this aligns well with our notes...

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 19 Dec 2026 09:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Post-call: on-prem inference requirements, network boundaries & next owners

Got it — thanks for the forward. Monica: can you own the POC estimate + the emergency patch workflow (deliverable: deployment-checklist.md with a 1-page risk summary) by 2026-12-23? I'll draft the high-level SOW and circulate for review on 2026-12-20; Camila, I'll loop you into that review.

If TheraHealth requires strictly zero outbound connections, our fallback is:
- USB-signed artifact delivery or air-gapped media import for patches (higher lead times and Ops cost).
- No real-time telemetry; only on-demand logs shipped via approved physical media or through their approved secure courier.

I'll note potential procurement impacts: longer POC schedule (adds ~3–4 weeks) and a modest ops premium for air-gapped servicing.

Ben Carter
Director, Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (650) 555-0198

title: Pricing Annex - packaging parity & escrow language (Sentinel Health redline)
preview:
From: Amy Park <amy.park@sentinelhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Chris Lowe <chris.lowe@sentinelhealth.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Dec 2026 09:14:00 -0500
Subject: Sentinel - Pricing Annex redline and packaging parity question

Ben — attaching our procurement redlines for the Pricing Annex and Order Schedule. High level asks: we want a firm cap on the escrow/reserve for usage swings, clarification on the caps/floors language (especially the rolling 90-day smoothing mechanism), and explicit packaging parity so we can migrate multi-product SKUs without price reopening.

Two specific callouts in the PDF: 1) Section 3.2 (Price Corridor) — the redline replaces the word \"calendar quarter\" with \"billing quarter\" and adds a hard cap at 20% for any interim adjustment; 2) Section 4.1 (Packaging Parity) — asks that changes to packaging that materially impact unit economics trigger a 30-day notice and price protection for 12 months.

Can you route to legal and let me know which items Redwood can accept, which need more discussion, and any practical alternatives you prefer? We reference our standard procurement exceptions at the back of the file.

Attachments: Pricing_Sentinel_Annex_Redline_12-16.pdf (redline, inline comments)

Thanks,
Amy Park
Procurement Manager, Sentinel Health
amy.park@sentinelhealth.com
+1 (415) 555-0193


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Amy Park <amy.park@sentinelhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 16 Dec 2026 12:45:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Sentinel - Pricing Annex redline and packaging parity question

Amy — thanks for sending this over and for the clear callouts. I've pulled together a short response and routed the redline to our legal/strategy leads (cc'd). High-level: we can probably accept a hard cap on escrow/reserve subject to a definition tweak (to limit to net-new usage volatility only), but the packaging parity language as written creates operational risk for us unless we add a materiality threshold and an exemption for model-repackaging that doesn't change unit compute.

Next steps: Karthik will propose specific alternate language for the corridor/escrow cap and Marissa will confirm operational tolerances for packaging changes. I'll get back to you with a consolidated markup by tomorrow afternoon.

Attaching a one-page summary for your procurement team (Ben_Responses_12-16.pdf).

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (650) 555-0177


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Amy Park <amy.park@sentinelhealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Dec 2026 11:02:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Sentinel - Pricing Annex redline and packaging parity question

Amy / Ben — Karthik here from Redwood's contracts and pricing team. I reviewed the redline and left an alternate markup (attached) that I think addresses Sentinel's commercial needs while preserving predictable economics for both sides. Key points below (quoted blocks reference the PDF comments):

> Amy wrote: \"Replace 'calendar quarter' with 'billing quarter' and cap interim adjustments at 20%\"

Proposed: We can accept 'billing quarter' but propose the cap apply to adjustments driven solely by delta in metered token consumption attributable to newly onboarded endpoints (net-new usage). Language: \"Interim adjustments shall not exceed 20% of the fee for the affected SKU in any billing quarter, and only when the adjustment results from net-new usage introduced subsequent to the Effective Date.\" This prevents oscillation from seasonal variance.

> Amy wrote: \"Packaging Parity: changes that materially impact unit economics trigger 30-day notice and 12-month protection\"

Proposed compromise: Add a materiality threshold of >=15% change in unit compute cost or end-to-end price to qualify. Also add a carve-out for repackaging that does not increase per-unit inference cost by >=15%. We included suggested language in redline_markup_v2.docx.

Open question for your team: what cadence do you require for true-ups (monthly vs quarterly)? Our standard is quarterly true-ups for committed accounts; we can be flexible to quarterly for year 1 and then annual true-ups thereafter if that helps get to signature. Please confirm.

Attachment: redline_markup_v2.docx (proposed edits inline)

--
Karthik Iyer
Director, Pricing Strategy — Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com


From: Amy Park <amy.park@sentinelhealth.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Chris Lowe <chris.lowe@sentinelhealth.com>
Date: Thu, 17 Dec 2026 15:30:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Sentinel - Pricing Annex redline and packaging parity question

Karthik — thanks for the fast, detailed reply and the proposed language. The 'net-new usage' carve-out for the 20% cap is reasonable; I will confirm internally how Sentinel defines 'net-new' but I expect we can align. The 15% materiality threshold for packaging parity is acceptable in principle but Procurement will want explicit examples in the SOW appendix — e.g., a SKU split that reroutes traffic between small/large models would trigger parity while a cosmetic SKU label change would not.

On true-up cadence: Procurement prefers monthly visibility with quarterly reconciliation. We ask that Redwood provide monthly billing detail and that only the quarterly reconciliation affect committed tiers. Is that operationally feasible? If we can align on monthly visibility + quarterly binding true-up, Procurement is prepared to proceed to signature quickly. We have a DocuSign packet ready to circulate when language is agreed: https://www.docusign.com/sentinel/packet/1a2b3c4d

Amy


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Amy Park <amy.park@sentinelhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 18 Dec 2026 18:05:00 -05:00
Subject: Final: Pricing annex — agreed edits and next steps

Amy / Chris — appreciate the quick turnaround. We reviewed internally and can accept the proposed compromises with two practical guardrails: 1) Quarterly binding true-ups for committed tiers (we will provide monthly usage reports for visibility as requested, with reconciliation applied at quarter close); 2) Escrow/reserve cap at 20% is acceptable if limited to net-new usage and with a defined lookback to avoid double-counting spikes (we propose a 90-day lookback window). Also accept the 15% materiality threshold for packaging parity with the SOW appendix examples to be captured in the Order Schedule.

To expedite: attached is an updated OrderSchedule_v3.xlsx and a consolidated Final_Redline_Package_12-18.pdf. If this reflects Sentinel's intent, we can have the DocuSign packet updated and pushed for signature by close of next week. HubSpot link for the deal is here: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/42098/DEAL-2026-0421

Attachments: OrderSchedule_v3.xlsx, Final_Redline_Package_12-18.pdf

Thanks — looking forward to closing this out.

Best,
Ben
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (650) 555-0177

Hi Sanjay / Elena —

Great to wrap up the contract signatures today (SOW_v3 attached). To get you into staging and aligned on the first sprint (Sprint 0), I’m sending our baseline staging checklist and a short MAP to confirm responsibilities.

High level: proposed go-live window target = 2027-02-01 to 2027-02-08 (dependent on integration testing). Key asks for Sprint 0:
- Staging environment access (SSH creds + workspace URL)
- Test data subset (PII-scrubbed sample of 200 records)
- API spec for their auth flow (OAuth / token exchange)
- SOC2 evidence request (listed below)

Owners (tentative):

- Customer success / onboarding: Marissa Cole (cc’d) — adoption and training plan owner
- Sales & escalation: Ben Carter — single-threaded contact for contract/terms and exec escalations
- Technical lead (MediTrek): Sanjay Patel — will provide API spec and staging credentials

Attachments: mutual_action_plan.xlsx (MAP) and SOW_v3.pdf (signed). Please review the MAP and confirm owners for any items that differ on your side. I’ve also included a quick staging checklist in the MAP.

Next steps: can you confirm staging access ETA and whether your security team requires an NDA or additional paperwork for SOC2 artifacts? If fine, suggest an intake call Wed 12/08 (30 min) to hand off technical details.

Thanks — excited to get moving.

—
Ben Carter

From: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Shah <samir.shah@bridgepoint.health>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: BridgePoint private connectivity orchestration pulse

Samir,

Thanks for the kickoff call earlier — putting a short pulse here to capture next steps for the private deployment (VPC peering, private endpoints, and on‑prem appliance planning). Goal is to agree on owners and a short two‑week implementation sprint plan.

Immediate asks:
- Can you confirm the VPC CIDR range you want us to peer with and whether you require Azure ExpressRoute or AWS Direct Connect?
- Please share any firewall egress allowlist constraints for our control plane IPs.
- Who on your side owns the network change window (name + email)?

Internal owners I've assigned:
- Rafael Mendes: networking runbook and peering ops
- Grace Oconnor: private endpoint routing and health checks
- Monica Patel: on‑prem appliance staging and install checklist
- Aditya Rao: rollout cadence and customer comms

Attachments: network‑diagram‑v1.pdf (draft), implementation‑checklist.xlsx (working). I'll follow up with a proposed 90‑minute deep‑dive workshop next week.

Thanks,
Michael

--
Michael Grant
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
michael.grant@redwoodinference.com
+1 415 555 0123

(attachment stubs: network-diagram-v1.pdf, implementation-checklist.xlsx)

From: Samir Shah <samir.shah@bridgepoint.health>
To: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Tracy Lin <tracy@netswitch.net>, James Park <j.park@networkops.io>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 11:09:00 -0800
Subject: Re: BridgePoint private connectivity orchestration pulse

Michael — thanks, confirming a few things from our side:

- VPC: 10.200.0.0/16 (primary), we will create a peering VPC in the same region.
- Connectivity: preference is AWS Direct Connect (we have an existing DX connection at us-west-2). If Redwood needs ExpressRoute, we can discuss but that will add procurement time.
- Firewall egress: allowlist for control plane: 52.23.14.101/32, 52.23.14.102/32, 52.23.14.103/32 — Tracy will confirm once her team verifies.
- Network change owner: Tracy Lin (tracy@netswitch.net) — she'll coordinate calendar windows.

A few questions for your team:
1) Do you require a dedicated/private endpoint in our VPC or can we proxy via a peered subnet?
2) Appliance: do you ship an OVA, or is it a containerized appliance we run on our infra?

We can do the workshop on Thurs 12/17 10:00–11:30 PT — available then?

Samir

On Tue, Dec 15, 2026 at 9:22 AM Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Samir,
>
> Thanks for the kickoff call earlier — putting a short pulse here to capture next steps for the private deployment...


From: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>
To: Samir Shah <samir.shah@bridgepoint.health>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Tracy Lin <tracy@netswitch.net>, James Park <j.park@networkops.io>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 13:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: BridgePoint private connectivity orchestration pulse

Thanks Samir — that works. Quick answers:

- Private endpoint vs proxy: we recommend a VPC‑native private endpoint (interface endpoint) to avoid double‑NAT and simplify health checks; RFC attached in the Drive link. If you prefer a peered subnet proxy, we can support that with a small perf caveat (added latency for NAT).
- Appliance: we provide an OVA and a container image for our on‑prem package. For your use case we suggest the OVA for dedicated appliances; Monica will share the appliance checklist and the required cert formats.

Rafael / Grace / Monica — can you bring the following to the Thurs workshop?
- Rafael: peering runbook draft + BGP practice notes (if we do DX)
- Grace: endpoint routing diagram + required health check ports
- Monica: appliance install checklist + cert requirements document

Proposed agenda for 12/17 10:00–11:30 PT (workshop):
1) Networking handoff & CIDR/BGP decisions (20m)
2) Private endpoint sizing and health checks (20m)
3) Appliance staging & install walk‑through (25m)
4) Cutover window and rollback plan (15m)

I'll send the calendar invite and include a short pre‑work packet.

Best,
Michael


From: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Samir Shah <samir.shah@bridgepoint.health>
Cc: Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Tracy Lin <tracy@netswitch.net>, James Park <j.park@networkops.io>
Date: Wed, 16 Dec 2026 08:16:00 -0800
Subject: Re: BridgePoint private connectivity orchestration pulse

Samir / team — prepping the peering runbook and attaching a compact checklist for the workshop. Key notes:

- If we use DX with public VIF + BGP, we recommend the following BGP community tags to preserve route propagation: 65530:100 / 65530:200.
- Health check ports: 443 (control), 5222 (internal service RPC), 12345 (custom telemetry) — Grace will confirm exact ranges.
- Change window estimate: 2 hours for route propagation and validation if pre‑staged.

Attachment: implementation‑checklist.xlsx (sheet: peering‑ops) — I've also included a brief staged rollback plan.

Suggestion: run the Workshop as a hybrid session with a short lab (appliance boot and health check) — Monica, can you stage an OVA snapshot and credentials for the lab? If yes, please attach the OVA pointer (we won't attach binary here).

-Rafael

(attachment stub: implementation-checklist.xlsx)

From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwoodinference.com>, Michael Grant <michael.grant@redwoodinference.com>, Samir Shah <samir.shah@bridgepoint.health>

Date: Thu, 17 Dec 2026 14:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: BridgePoint private connectivity orchestration pulse

All — workshop confirmed for today 10:00 PT (calendar invite sent). Quick pre‑notes:

- OVA: I have an OVA snapshot staged in our S3 bucket and will provide a pointer during the lab. We require the appliance to have the following certs in PEM format: TLS server cert, client cert for control plane, and a signing cert for package verification. See appliance‑cert‑requirements.docx attached.
- Pre‑work: please review the peering runbook (Rafael) and the endpoint sizing doc (Grace) before the call.
- Deliverables from the workshop: signed mutual action plan, peering config snippets, and a tentative cutover window. I'll draft the MAP in HubSpot and share the link after the workshop.

Thanks — looking forward to the lab.

Monica

--
Monica Patel
Implementation Lead, Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwoodinference.com

- Private endpoint vs proxy: we recommend a VPC‑native private endpoint (interface endpoint) to avoid double‑NAT and simplify health checks; RFC attached in the Drive link. If you prefer a peered subnet proxy, we can support that with a small perf caveat (added latency for NAT).
- Appliance: we provide an OVA and a container image for our on‑prem package. For your use case we suggest the OVA for dedicated appliances; Monica will share the appliance checklist and the required cert formats.

Rafael / Grace / Monica — can you bring the following to the Thurs workshop?
- Rafael: peering runbook draft + BGP practice notes (if we do DX)
- Grace: endpoint routing diagram + required health check ports
- Monica: appliance install checklist + cert requirements document

Proposed agenda for 12/17 10:00–11:30 PT (workshop):
1) Networking handoff & CIDR/BGP decisions (20m)
2) Private endpoint sizing and health checks (20m)
3) Appliance staging & install walk‑through (25m)
4) Cutover window and rollback plan (15m)

I'll send the calendar invite and include a short pre‑work packet.

Best,
Michael

- Change window estimate: 2 hours for route propagation and validation if pre‑staged.

title: Renewal: Aurelia Health — Premium SLA & Capacity increase options
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aurelia Health Procurement <procurement@aurelia.health>, Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@aurelia.health>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 29 Nov 2026 15:03:00 -0800
Subject: Renewal: Aurelia Health — premium SLA and capacity options

Hi Sarah / procurement team —

Thanks again for the partnership this year. Your renewal is coming up Jan 3, 2027 and I wanted to surface a couple of options we discussed in Q3: upgrading to our Premium SLA (faster tail-latency SLOs, 24/7 priority on-call) and reserving a dedicated burst pool to guarantee capacity during peak forecast windows.

Bottom line / asks:
- Would Aurelia be open to a 12-month commitment with Premium SLA (target 95th percentile token latency SLO of 120ms and 99.9% uptime) in exchange for a blended unit price credit versus on-demand?
- Do you have updated peak throughput projections for Jan–Mar 2027 so we can size reserved capacity?

I attached a short proposal with two lanes: Premium SLA + 10% committed capacity and Premium SLA + 20% committed capacity (with tiered credits). If it helps, we can do a 60-day staged rollout and a canary route for critical endpoints.

I’ll loop Lauren (renewals) to run the commercial side and Marissa for executive approval if we need an SLO exception.

Next steps: quick call this week to align on capacity numbers and preferred SLA lane, then I’ll send a formal addendum for signature.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0192

Attachments: Aurelia_Renewal_Proposal_Q4-27.pdf (proposal), SLA_Addendum_v3.docx (addendum draft)

From: Sarah Kim <sarah.kim@aurelia.health>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@aurelia.health>
Date: Mon, 30 Nov 2026 09:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Renewal: Aurelia Health — premium SLA and capacity options

Ben —

Thanks for the note and the materials. We are definitely interested in the Premium SLA but need to validate budget and internal approvals. A couple quick items:

- Our current Q1 forecast shows a ~35% bump in peak tokens for payer-auth flows (mostly 08:00–10:00 PST). I can share the spreadsheet with hourly peaks.
- Procurement will need the legal redlines around data retention and audit logs for the addendum. Can you confirm the addendum includes SOC2 evidence links and a retention clause?
- Pricing: can you provide a banded example (baseline vs. 10% commit vs. 20% commit) in the proposal? The attached pdf has helpful detail but a 1-page pricing table would be ideal for our CFO.

Tom will coordinate procurement review. Let us know available times this week for a 30-min sync.

Best,
Sarah
Sarah Kim
Head of Platform Operations, Aurelia Health

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 01 Dec 2026 10:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: Renewal: Aurelia Health — premium SLA and capacity options

Folks — forwarding Sarah’s reply. Key asks: 1) pricing band in 1-pager, 2) confirmation that the addendum includes SOC2 links + retention language, 3) sizing guidance for a burst pool to handle ~35% peak bump.

My recommended approach:
- Offer a two-tier commit (10% / 20%) with step-down credits for overage; present simple table so the CFO can sign off quickly.
- Include SOC2 link and a standard 90-day retention clause; procurement rarely pushes for bespoke retention but they asked so flagging to legal if they do.
- Propose a 60-day staged canary for high-risk endpoints and a guaranteed burst pool sized to handle the 35% uplift (we can propose a conservative buffer).

Marissa — if we want to offer any one-time onboarding credit or shorter term to close, now is the time to decide.

Ben

Thanks Ben — this is aligned with our standard renewal plays. I reviewed the attached addendum and a few notes:

- Pricing band: okay to present 10% and 20% commit lanes. Suggest we show forecasted monthly spend and effective unit price to keep it simple. Evelyn, can you prepare the one-pager pricing table (2 lanes + baseline)?
- Legal/retention: standard SOC2 link is fine. For retention, we should propose a default 90-day retention with an option to extend at customer cost; avoid changing platform defaults unless procurement insists.
- SLO language: include explicit 95th and 99th percentile token latency metrics and a playbook for on-call escalation — that reduces ambiguity.

Marissa — for commercial flexibility: we can offer a one-time onboarding credit equal to one month of committed spend if Aurelia signs both the Premium SLA and 12-month commit by Dec 22. This closes the loop quickly and helps procurement. Approve?

Lauren

title: MAP: Unit cost verification & evaluation handoff
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Sara Holt <sara.holt@helixmed.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: MAP: Unit cost verification & evaluation handoff

Sara / team —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our mutual action plan, we promised a short, actionable handoff that locks the cost assumptions for the pilot evaluation so procurement and engineering can sign off. Attaching a condensed MAP + the simulation CSV with our baseline assumptions. High level summary below: 

- Tokens / $: baseline use case (candidate generation + light rerank) assumed 0.012 tokens/$ on Redwood hosted with 8k context using quantized gptx-4q. We modeled 3 traffic profiles in unit-cost-sims.csv (steady, peak, burst).
- Caching assumptions: prefix caching for 55% of requests (session-based similarity) with a 7-day TTL; cache hit reduces downstream token usage by ~62% for matching queries.
- Batching: continuous batching enabled for 40–60ms latency budget on p99; we used a conservative 1.3x token-efficiency uplift at 25QPS and 1.7x uplift at 100QPS in the sims.

Ask: can procurement confirm the target units-per-dollar threshold for pilot approval (we flagged two gates in the MAP: finance threshold and SLA-backed latency)? If procurement wants an alternative pricing construct (reserved vs burst credits) we can run the sim again and include a short cost delta table.

Next steps I propose: 
1) You confirm the procurement threshold and whether reserved capacity is on the table (by EOD 12/16).
2) Ben & Laura will prepare a one-page cost narrative tying the MAP milestones to the unit economics checks for your CFO.
3) Redwood will run a quick reserved-vs-hosted sim if reserved is selected (deliverable: delta table + updated MAP).

Attachments: map-eval-plan-v2.pdf (MAP + timeline), unit-cost-sims.csv (traffic sims), redwood-prefetch-batching-notes.txt (internal ops notes).

Thanks —
Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Strategic Accounts, Redwood Inference



From: Sara Holt <sara.holt@helixmed.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@helixmed.com, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 11:04:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: MAP: Unit cost verification & evaluation handoff

On Tue, 15 Dec 2026 at 09:12, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com> wrote:
> Sara / team —
> 
> Thanks again for the time yesterday. Per our mutual action plan...

Thanks Karthik — this is exactly what we needed. A couple quick clarifications from procurement: 

- The CFO asked for tokens/$ expressed as effective tokens per USD after caching and batching at expected pilot traffic. Can you include the final "effective tokens/$" row in the delta table? That will make the ask binary for sign-off.
- For the pilot our target effective tokens/$ needs to be <= 9,000 tokens per dollar (after caching & batching). That’s the current internal benchmark; if your sim shows a different baseline we’ll need to push on the pricing or reduce per-request generation.
- Reserved capacity: procurement is open to a three-month reserved bucket if the unit delta is >15% better than hosted.

One more: can Ben join the technical call tomorrow to walk the eng team through the prefetch/caching implications? Laura — looping you for calendar.

Sara
Sara Holt
PM, HelixMed



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Sara Holt <sara.holt@helixmed.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 15 Dec 2026 13:21:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: MAP: Unit cost verification & evaluation handoff

Thanks Sara — I can run the call and cover the prefetch/caching tradeoffs. Quick notes to align before the call so the demo is tight:

- Effective tokens/$: in the sims I included raw tokens consumed by model runtime and removed cached-prefix tokens. I’ll add an explicit column named "effective_tokens_per_usd" that factors caching hits and the batching uplift. Expect two rows: hosted baseline and reserved-3mo baseline.
- If your target is <=9k tokens/$ we’ll likely need either stronger prefix cache hit (>70%) or a larger batching window (up to 80ms p99) or move to a quantization profile that trades a small quality delta for 20–30% token-cost improvement. I’ll surface sample prompts that are sensitive to that trade.
- Engineering ask for the call: please share a small sample of 200 typical user prompts (or synthetic proxy) so we can run a quick prefix-match cover test.

I’ll update the CSV and push a v2 with the effective_tokens_per_usd column before the call. Tentative call: Wed 12/16 10:00 PT.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Sara Holt <sara.holt@helixmed.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, procurement@helixmed.com
Date: Wed, 16 Dec 2026 14:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: MAP: Unit cost verification & evaluation handoff

Sara / procurement team —

Appreciate the clarity. Quick confirmation of the MAP handoff outcomes after Ben's update: 

- Deliverables by EOD 12/16: unit-cost-sims.csv (with effective_tokens_per_usd), one-page cost narrative (Ben/Laura), and an updated MAP PDF that shows the finance gate tied to the <=9k effective tokens/$ metric.
- Meeting Wed 12/16 10:00 PT to review prefetch/caching callouts — Ben will lead; please send sample prompts to ben@redwood.com ahead of the call.
- If reserved-3mo is requested after the sim, Redwood will deliver a reserved-vs-hosted delta table within 48 hours.

I’ll mark the MAP milestone "finance gate: unit-econ signoff" as conditional on the effective_tokens_per_usd row meeting your <=9k threshold or on procurement selecting reserved-3mo with a >15% delta.

If we’re aligned I’ll update the HubSpot MAP record and attach the artifacts.

Thanks —
Karthik

title: Geo-anchoring & Scheduled Purge: confirmation needed for SummitTech Dedicated
preview:
From: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0500
Subject: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our security & infra team has one final set of questions before we sign the SOW around data residency and deletion for the Dedicated cluster you proposed. In particular: 

- Can you confirm which physical country the SummitTech Dedicated cluster will be pinned to (both primary host and backups)?
- What is Redwood's standard timeline and steps for a tenant-scoped purge (requested by customer)? Please include snapshot, backup, and object-store flows.
- Do you provide a post-purge attestation (signed) and sample logs that show the erase events?

We need this information to close our internal review and provide an acceptance note to procurement. If you can send a short runbook and any artifacts (attestation template/sample logs), we can sign off.

Appreciate a response by EOD tomorrow US Eastern if possible.

Best,
Aisha Rana
Security Program Manager
SummitTech

---
Attachment: none


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 11:30:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Hi Aisha — thanks for the clear list. Quick answers up front, then I’ll attach the runbook artifact for your review.

1) Country pinning: we will provision your Dedicated pool in the US-East (Virginia) region and anchor tenant data to that region. Backups/snapshots will also be stored in the same legal jurisdiction; cross-region replication is not enabled for this Dedicated contract unless you opt in.

2) Purge timeline & steps (customer-initiated tenant purge):
   - Day 0 (request received): We acknowledge and open a purge ticket, schedule maintenance window if needed.
   - Hours 0–2: Quiesce incoming writes to the tenant and take a final snapshot flagged for retention window zero.
   - Hours 2–6: Execute KV cache flush and remove live tenant objects; initiate snapshot deletion workflow (primary storage) and mark tenant metadata for wipe.
   - Hours 6–24: Verify object-store deletions and start metadata tombstone sweep.
   - Day 2–7: Run forensic verification against artifact indexes and purge any remaining ephemeral caches and worker local state.
   - Up to Day 30: We maintain logical retention for audit purposes only; we can shorten this window by mutual agreement.

3) Attestation & logs: we provide a signed attestation PDF (template attached) and an extract of erase-event logs showing erase-job IDs, timestamps, and operator confirmation. Sample log extract is attached as JSON.

I’ve attached a short "Country Commitment and Purge Runbook" (jurisdiction-commitment.pdf) and a sample erase log (redwood-erase-log-sample.json). Priya is on copy to add technical detail for snapshot/backups and the playbook diagram.

If those artifacts look good, we can produce a formal signed attestation and a link to the deletion evidence at the run of the purge.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Customer Solutions
Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- jurisdiction-commitment.pdf (PDF, 2 pages)
- redwood-erase-log-sample.json (JSON, 1.2KB)


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Wed, 15 Dec 2026 10:05:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Hi Aisha — quick technical clarifications to Ben's plan so expectations are aligned. I’ll keep this short and operational.

- Snapshots: we take point-in-time snapshots at the storage layer. For Dedicated customers who request zero-retention purge, we mark the snapshot as "expire-immediate" and trigger storage-provider delete API calls. Note: object-store providers may have internal deletion windows (finalized deletes can vary by provider, we surface the delete timestamp and job id).

- Backups & replicas: by default there is no cross-region replication. If SummitTech later requests an asynchronous geo-replica, that would create additional retention vectors and need explicit removal steps.

- Forensic verification: our purge job produces a 3-part artifact: 1) erase-event log (job id, worker nodes, timestamps), 2) snapshot delete receipts from object-store, 3) a short verification checklist run by ops engineers (hash checks for tombstone markers). We bundle those into the attestation PDF.

- Sample timeline nuance: in practice most tenant purges complete within 24 hours (live objects + metadata), but final sweep/forensic wrap-up can extend to 7 days for complex workloads. If you require a hard SLA (e.g., all artifacts produced within 72 hours), we can add that to the contract as an SLA exception.

Attached is the purge workflow diagram that maps actions to storage and worker components. Also looping in Karthik for any legal/backup replication notes that should be surfaced in the attestation template.

Thanks — Priya
Priya Natarajan
Product Security Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments:
- purge-workflow-diagram.png (PNG, 860KB)


From: Aisha Rana <aisha.rana@summittech.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Dan Cho <dan.cho@summittech.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Dec 2026 14:08:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Geo-anchoring and purge timeline for our Dedicated instance

Ben/Priya — this is perfect and addresses our checklist. SummitTech accepts the proposed country anchoring to US-East and the purge workflow as described. A few final asks: 

1) Please produce the signed attestation PDF (use the attached template) once the SOW is countersigned. We need the attestation to be signed by an authorized Redwood security representative.
2) Please add an SLA clause that the final forensic artifact bundle (erase-event log + object-store receipts + verification checklist) will be delivered within 72 hours of purge request. We will take that as a contractual acceptance criterion.
3) Share a Drive folder link for evidence delivery and the initial sample artifacts you attached earlier. Dan will coordinate procurement and include the attestation requirement in the SOW.

If you can confirm (1) and (2) by EOD tomorrow, we’ll move the SOW to countersignature. Thanks for the quick turn here.

Best,
Aisha Rana
Security Program Manager
SummitTech

---
Included artifacts referenced: jurisdiction-commitment.pdf, redwood-erase-log-sample.json, purge-workflow-diagram.png
