> [previous message omitted]



From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.inference>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>
Date: Fri, 04 Dec 2026 11:40:00 -0800
Subject: Internal: iCare questions — draft answers and artifacts to send

Aditya / Marcus — internal note with concise answers we can share with iCare. Please flag anything you want edited before I hand this off. I recommend sending the attestation + BAA draft and offering a technical deep-dive.

Short answers for the customer:
- Hosted + BAA: Yes. Our Hosted API can be covered by an industry-standard BAA. We can offer dedicated tenancy and VPC peering for extra isolation; however, Hosted shared-control-plane customers are logically separated. Customer-managed KMS is available for Dedicated and Private; Hosted supports Bring-Your-Own-Key (BYOK) in specific regions — would need a short enablement window.
- Log retention / redaction: Default request logs retained 90 days. We can support configurable retention down to 30 days for PHI-sensitive customers. Redaction: we provide configurable request/response redaction policies and automated redaction helpers; we can also export sanitized logs for audits.
- VPC & private IPs: Dedicated supports private networking (VPC peering, private endpoints) and subnets in customer-owned VPCs. Private deployment (VPC or on-prem) offers full network isolation and control.
- Audit evidence: attached index maps evidence artifacts to SOC 2 control categories. For an audit we can provide copies of control operation evidence (change logs, vulnerability scans, access reviews) under an NDA / audit arrangement.
- Subprocessors: attached list; we are transparent about cloud infra providers and managed service partners.

Attachments included for outbound to iCare:
- Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_Attestation_2025.pdf (redacted where appropriate)
- Redwood_BAA_Draft_v2.docx (starter BAA)
- network_diagram_vpc_peering.png (illustrates hosted with VPC peering and dedicated capacity)
- audit_evidence_index.csv (mapping of evidence items to SOC 2 criteria)

Suggested outbound language: share the attestation + BAA, call out the options (Hosted+BAA for quickest path; Dedicated or Private if they need stronger isolation), propose tech deep-dive on Dec 10, and loop procurement.

Vanessa Ortiz
Security & Compliance Manager | Redwood Inference
vanessa@redwood.inference



From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.inference>
To: Samantha Lee <samantha.lee@icare.health>, Aaron Patel <aaron.patel@icare.health>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.inference>
Date: Sat, 12 Dec 2026 09:05:00 -0800
Subject: iCare — evidence & next steps (SOC2, BAA draft, technical deep-dive)

Samantha / Aaron — attaching the items we discussed and proposed next steps. Thanks again for the transparency on requirements.

Attachments included: Redwood_SOC2_TypeII_Attestation_2025.pdf, Redwood_BAA_Draft_v2.docx, network_diagram_vpc_peering.png, audit_evidence_index.csv

Short answers to your bullets:
- Hosted & BYOK: Hosted can be HIPAA-enabled under a signed BAA. BYOK (customer-managed KMS) is supported for Dedicated and Private by default; for Hosted we can enable BYOK for eligible regions — please flag if iCare requires KMS in-scope immediately.
- Log retention: default 90 days; we can set retention to 30 days for PHI-sensitive streams and provide sanitized exports for audits (CSV/S3 or SIEM integration).
- Dedicated networking: Dedicated supports VPC peering, private endpoints, and private IP addressing for endpoints. Private (VPC/on-prem) is also available if you need full control over the control plane.

Proposed next steps:
1) Review attached attestation + BAA draft. We can push the BAA into DocuSign if the legal language looks acceptable (DocuSign link: https://docusign.example/icare/BAA-ICARE-1234).
2) Technical deep-dive: Vanessa and Marcus can run a short architecture session for your infra team — suggested slots Dec 16 or Dec 17 morning PST.
3) Procurement: Lauren (cc'd) is ready to pick up timelines and terms once the BAA is agreed. Please share any procurement templates you want us to fill.

If helpful, we can start with Hosted + signed BAA for an initial proof-of-concept and migrate to Dedicated/Private for production. That path shortens time-to-value while meeting contractual obligations.

Open questions for you so we can prepare the deep-dive:
- Do you require CloudHSM-backed keys or are KMS-managed keys acceptable?
- Any specific SIEM or audit log schema we should prepare (Splunk, Sumo Logic, etc.)?

Thanks — will await confirmation on the deep-dive slot.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.inference

- Goal: reduce inference cost and 95th-pct latency for Bluewave’s table extraction pipeline (current ops: 3x GPUs, p95 ~320ms).
- Proposed immediate play: pilot Redwood Dedicated on inferred workload with KV-cache + per-route batching to test latency gains and cost delta.

title: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood-inference.com>
To: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Tue, 7 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment

Hi Liam —

Thanks for taking the call earlier. Attached is an initial slab model (initial-slab-model.xlsx) that maps rough tokens/sec requirements to GPU slabs under a couple of modeling assumptions. High level: we built three scenarios (Conservative / Expected / Aggressive) using your ballpark numbers from the demo.

Quick asks so we can lock numbers:
- 95th% concurrent inference sessions during peak 10-minute windows (rough count)
- Avg tokens per request (our model needs both prompt + response avg)
- Peak hours (timezone/day) and whether peaks are predictable or ad-hoc
- Target latency SLO (p95) you want to guarantee for the commitment

Key modeling assumptions in the sheet (to review):
- Serving model: picking a production-optimized variant (redwood-3.1b-qat) as baseline
- Quantization: int8 path for committed slabs
- Batching: conservative batching window = 10ms; optimistic = 30ms
- KV cache hit assumed = 45% for chat workloads (affects effective tokens/sec)

Ballpark numbers from the conservative column: ~38k tokens/sec sustained across the committed slab (with a 1.4x headroom factor for bursts). The spreadsheet contains per-GPU effective tokens/sec based on our microbenchmarks.

I included Ben on this thread — he'll call out a recommendation for headroom and failover allocation. If you can share the short profile above by end of day Thursday we can iterate and push a formal slab recommendation + an ops runbook for cold-starts and cache warmups.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood-inference.com | +1 (415) 555-0172

Attachment: initial-slab-model.xlsx (Excel, 120KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood-inference.com>, Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com>
Date: Tue, 7 Dec 2026 12:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment

Adding a few modeling clarifications / assumptions from infra side:

- Model profile: if SpectrumLytics plans to use a 7B class (quantized) vs a 70B class there is ~4-6x difference in effective tokens/s per GPU. Please confirm target family.
- Kernel optimizations: we modeled int8 quant paths; fp16 paths require ~20-30% more GPU time per token.
- Batching/window: effective tokens/sec jumps non-linearly with a 20–40ms batching allowance; if you cannot accept additional latency for batching we drop into lower throughput per GPU.
- KV cache: recommend instrumenting a short warmup period in canary to validate predicted 40–60% cache hit rate.

Recommendation for committed headroom: plan for a 1.5x safety factor on top of expected sustained tokens/sec for Q1 (this buys capacity for seasonality and burst patterns). For burst absorption (unplanned peaks) we recommend a 2-node hot standby pool in a different AZ to preserve failover SLA.

I attached a short note with compute-footprint assumptions. If you want, we can run a quick synthetic that maps your sample trace to GPU-days and tokens/month.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Capacity Planning | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood-inference.com

Attachment: compute-footprint-notes.pdf (PDF, 45KB)

From: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood-inference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Wed, 8 Dec 2026 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment

Hi Ava / Ben —

Thanks for the model. Apologies for the slow reply — procurement + security had follow-ups. Here are the data points you asked for (short answers):
- 95th% concurrent sessions (10-min window): ~320 sessions
- Avg tokens per request (prompt + response): 420 tokens
- Peak windows: Mon–Fri 09:00–11:00 PT and 17:00–19:00 PT; predictable but with occasional spikes around campaign launches
- Target p95 latency SLO: 350ms (we can accept up to 400ms during known campaigns)

Additional notes: our legal team prefers a two-step procurement: (1) pilot month with reduced commit, (2) 12-month dedicated commitment after pilot. Procurement referenced HubSpot deal SL-2026-042 and sent a DocuSign to contracts@spectrumlytics.com.

If you can translate the numbers into a minimal committed slab for the pilot (supporting predictable peaks with transparent burst buffer), that would help us accelerate internal approvals.

On the model family: we plan to run a mix of medium 13B quantized variants for conversational agents and a small set of 70B for complex summarization calls (~10% of traffic).

Thanks,
Liam O'Neil
VP Product | SpectrumLytics
liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com

-----Original Message-----
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood-inference.com>
Sent: Tue, Dec 7, 2026 at 09:12 AM
To: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>
Subject: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment

(quoted for context)

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood-inference.com>
To: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@spectrumlytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood-inference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood-inference.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Dec 2026 15:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Sizing proposal and headroom for SpectrumLytics Dedicated commitment

Liam —

Thanks for the details; that profile is very helpful. Below is a concise recommendation tailored for the pilot -> annual commit path you described. I’ve attached a final slab recommendation spreadsheet that converts your token profile into per-GPU counts and an ops checklist for cold-starts / cache warmups.

Executive recommendation (pilot -> commit):
- Pilot (30 days): 3x H100-equivalent GPUs in the dedicated pool + 1x A100 for canary; configured with int8 quant path for 13B family and fp16 fallback for 70B calls. Expected sustainable throughput (pilot config): ~12–14k tokens/sec across the pool with ability to burst to ~22k tokens/sec for short windows using short-lived burst nodes.
- Annual committed slab (recommended): 10x H100-equivalent GPUs (primary slab) + 2x A100 for cross-AZ failover. With a 1.5x headroom factor this maps to an expected sustained capacity of ~40–42k tokens/sec (covers regular peaks & seasonal growth).

Why this split: H100-class nodes give better peak token/s for your 13B quantized workload and improve latency for small-response chat flows. A100 reserve nodes are cheaper and act as durable failover for longer-running summarization jobs (70B flows) that we fall back to fp16 on.

Operational notes:
- Cold-starts & cache warmup: warm the KV cache with synthetic traffic in the first 48hrs of pilot; the ops checklist in the spreadsheet has exact sample curl sequences.
- Latency SLO mapping: with the pilot pool we expect p95 ~330–360ms for standard chat; with the annual slab p95 should be ~280–340ms depending on burst windows. We will instrument tracing and token-level cost breakdowns in the console.
- Billing/commit: we can phase the commit to start billing on day 31 for the annual slab if pilot metrics meet agreed thresholds.

Next steps I propose:
1) We lock the pilot slab and run a 48hr synthetic traffic run to validate our KV hit assumptions (can do this as early as next week).
2) If synth numbers match (within 10%), we provision the pilot and share a runbook and rollback plan.
3) Marissa, can you coordinate procurement artifacts and the DocuSign route?

Marissa will pull in contracts and finalize the DocuSign envelope. If you’re comfortable with this direction I’ll update the HubSpot deal with the proposed slab counts and attach the spreadsheet.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood-inference.com | +1 (415) 555-0172

Marissa Cole
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood-inference.com

Attachment: final-slab-recommendation.xlsx (Excel, 95KB)

title: Tiebreak & qualitative closure: Maya Ortiz (SWE IC)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Hannah Lin <hannah.lin@recruiting.co>
Date: 2026-12-09T09:02:00-08:00
Subject: Tiebreak & qualitative closure: Maya Ortiz (SWE IC)

Team — quick one: the panel scores for Maya landed tied between hire/hold and we need a tiebreak and any final qual notes to feed back to People Ops by EOD. Summary attached (scorecard + work sample).

Key points: 
- Algo interview: strong (Aditya gave 4.5/5) but flagged limited system-design depth at the 30–45 minute horizon.
- Coding: clean, edge cases handled, +2 for communication (Ava).
- Culture/ownership: mixed signals — some examples of follow-through, some gaps.

Ask: please reply with your final recommendation (hire/hold/pass), 1–2 sentence rationale, and any redlines or gaps we should record for the candidate file. If you recommend hire, flag if you want a conditional (mentorship/6mo review).

Attachments: Maya_Ortiz_scorecard.xlsx (xlsx), maya-ortiz-work-sample.pdf (pdf)

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Engineering Manager | Redwood Inference


From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-12-09T09:38:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Tiebreak & qualitative closure: Maya Ortiz (SWE IC)

Ben / all — I'm +1 hire. Short rationale: algorithmic thinking is solid, code style is production-ready, and she recovered well when pushed on complexity. My scores: Algo 4.5, Coding 4.0, Design 3.5, Culture 3.5 => weighted avg 3.9.

One note: design depth was limited because she focused on micro-optimizations early; with a 45–60m prompt she likely expands to system-level tradeoffs. Recommend hire with a 6-month design mentorship check-in (peer shadow + small design project).

--
Aditya Rao | Staff SWE | Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Hannah Lin <hannah.lin@recruiting.co>
Date: 2026-12-09T10:11:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Tiebreak & qualitative closure: Maya Ortiz (SWE IC)

Ben — I'm leaning hire. Rationale: coding exercise was very clean, clarity of thought under time was a +. Culture examples: ownership story around a bug fix was okay but not outstanding. If we hire, I prefer a small onboarding project and explicit expectations around cross-team follow-ups for the first 90 days.

Quick bullet qual notes: 
- Communication: 4/5 (clear, concise)
- Learning velocity: 4/5 (picked up unfamiliar lib quickly during work-sample)
- Autonomy: 3/5 (preferred close direction in ambiguity)

Recommend: Hire, with 90-day goals and mentorship pairing.

Ava
Senior SWE


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-12-09T11:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Tiebreak & qualitative closure: Maya Ortiz (SWE IC)

I put hold initially because of the culture/ownership uncertainty — after re-reading the work sample and the interview transcript I am willing to convert to hire if we put two conditions in the offer: 
1) 6-month performance review specifically to assess independent ownership on a cross-cutting bug/enhancement.
2) Clear onboarding success criteria documented in the offer packet.

If team is okay with those conditions I'll move my vote to hire.

Marissa
Director of Engineering, Hiring Manager


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-12-09T13:08:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Tiebreak & qualitative closure: Maya Ortiz (SWE IC)

+1 hire. Two quick observations from loop:
- Learning curve looks manageable given prior infra experience; expect faster ramp into service-level ownership than Marissa feared.
- No security/red-flag items in the background check draft Hannah shared.

I'll consolidate a short candidate summary for People Ops and tag the offer conditions you proposed.

title: Post-call: Trial onboarding plan & integration playbook (Stone Analytics)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@stoneanalytics.com>, Nina Gómez <nina.gomez@stoneanalytics.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 05 Dec 2026 15:02:00 -0800
Subject: Post-call: Trial onboarding plan & integration playbook (Stone Analytics)

Hi Samir / Nina — thanks again for the discovery call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment before kickoff: 

Summary of needs (from today)
- Real-time summarization for analyst notes (streaming output, sub-250ms per chunk)
- Embeddings index for semantic search across 18 months of reports (~200k documents)
- Tool calling to surface BI queries against their SQL layer and callouts to the document store

Success criteria we discussed
1) Latency: 95th perc token latency <= 300ms in our hosted trial environment
2) Throughput: support bursts up to 80 concurrent streaming sessions
3) Quality: key metric is F1 >= 0.78 on intent-extraction test set (we'll exchange test prompts)
4) Integration: SDK-based ingest + API streaming for summarization working end-to-end within the 4-week trial

Proposed trial plan (high level)
- Week 0 (this week): Share trial runbook + sample prompts, agree on test-set and credentials
- Week 1: SDK ingest + embeddings index build; validate sample queries
- Week 2: Streaming summarization + tool-calling connector to their SQL test DB; run initial evals
- Week 3: Iterate on prompt/templates, quantization recommendations; measure cost per 1k tokens
- Week 4: Final evaluation, handoff to CS for production planning or Dedicated sizing if it meets criteria

Owners
- Redwood technical lead (integration): Dylan Brooks
- Redwood runtime tuning & cost: Marcus Lin
- Customer PM: Samir Patel / Nina Gómez

Action items (immediate)
- Redwood: share the trial runbook + sample-api-spec (see attachments) — I attached the initial runbook and an API snippet.
- Stone: share test credentials (read-only SQL user + S3 sample access) and the evaluation prompt set by EOD Monday
- Schedule kickoff tech-sync: propose Tue 2026-12-08 09:00 PT (please confirm)

Attachments: stone-trial-tech-runbook.xlsx (workbook), sample-api-spec.md (snippet)

Thanks — happy to adjust timeline based on your ops window.

Best,
Ava Chen
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0124

---
Note: I included the streaming recommendation (chunk size 512 tokens, continuous KV caching) under the runbook tab.


From: Samir Patel <samir@stoneanalytics.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>
Cc: Nina Gómez <nina.gomez@stoneanalytics.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 07 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: Trial onboarding plan & integration playbook (Stone Analytics)

Hi Ava — appreciate the thorough recap. A few quick clarifications before we lock the kickoff: 

1) Test data: we can provision a read-only user to a replica DB and a subset S3 bucket (about ~60k docs) by Monday EOD. Is that sufficient for the trial?
2) Streaming: when you say 95th perc token latency <= 300ms, is that for a single GPU instance in Redwood hosted or for Dedicated? We want to understand if Hosted can meet the latency target for our user-facing app.
3) Tool calling: the BI queries surface will need a connector that can run parametrized SQL and return structured rows (CSV/JSON). Do you have a sample connector for Postgres we can test?

We can do Tue 12/8 09:00 PT for the kickoff. Nina will own the test prompts and will share the evaluation suite later today.

Thanks,
Samir
Lead Product Manager, Stone Analytics
samir@stoneanalytics.com

> On Sat, Dec 5, 2026 at 3:02 PM Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com> wrote:
> > Hi Samir / Nina — thanks again for the discovery call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment before kickoff: 
> > ...


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 08 Dec 2026 06:20:00 -08:00
Subject: Internal: Stone trial — tech split and checklist

Marcus / Ava — following Samir's questions and the runbook, I sketched a short implementation checklist and who does what. Please weigh in.

Implementation checklist (initial)
- Sandbox provisioning (Redwood): ephemeral hosted space with 2 vCPU-equivalents and 1x A100-like instance; endpoint + streaming enabled — Owner: Dylan (ETA: provision Tue 12/8 before kickoff)
- SDK ingest tool: provide node/python clients and a small ingestion script for S3 -> embeddings index (fast text encoder). Owner: Marcus (ETA: Wed 12/9)
- Postgres connector (tool calling): share sample connector (param SQL + JSON rows) and test harness. Owner: Dylan (ETA: Wed 12/9)
- Eval harness: template prompts + scoring script for F1/precision/recall. Owner: Ava (ETA: Thu 12/10)
- Cost telemetry: baseline run for token cost estimation and streaming increments. Owner: Marcus (ETA: week 2)

Replies to Samir's items
- Data volume: 60k docs is fine to validate indexing and query quality at trial scale. We'll align on shard sizing if we see hot shards.
- Latency target: the 300ms 95th is realistic in Redwood Hosted *with* streaming optimizations and continuous batching; we'll confirm with baseline numbers during week 1. If Stone needs Dedicated to meet production SLOs, we'll surface sizing by week 3.
- Postgres connector: I'll drop a sample in the shared Drive and include a minimal Docker-based harness that returns JSON rows for the agent to consume.

I'll provision sandbox and the connector artifacts right after the kickoff. Ava — can you share the eval prompt templates today so we can pre-seed the harness?

Thanks,
Dylan
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

---
Forwarded message: From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@stoneanalytics.com>, Nina Gómez <nina.gomez@stoneanalytics.com>
Cc: Dylan Brooks <dylan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 09 Dec 2026 09:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Post-call: Trial onboarding plan & integration playbook (Stone Analytics)

Samir / Nina — thanks, confirmed for Tue 12/8 09:00 PT (I added the calendar invite). A few attachments and links to get started are in the Drive: shared Drive folder: https://drive.company/drive/folders/stone-trial-resources

Attachments included with this thread:
- stone-trial-tech-runbook.xlsx (tabs: timeline, success-metrics, infra, api-examples)
- sample-api-spec.md (how we call the streaming endpoint + tool calling example)

Important notes for integration (excerpt)
- SDKs: prefer the Python client for ingestion (example command: redwood ingest --source s3://stone-sample --batch 512 --encoder fast-text)
- Streaming: client opens streaming session, sends chunks up to 512 tokens, Redwood returns incremental deltas and tool invocation hooks in JSONL events (see sample-api-spec.md)
- Tool calling: agent should emit an action: {"tool":"postgres-query","params":{...}} — Redwood will surface the action to the connector which returns rows; connector must return a JSON array with keys matching the tool schema.

Next steps (short checklist)
1) Stone: share DB creds + S3 sample by EOD Mon 12/7 (Samir) — status?
2) Redwood: Dylan to provision sandbox and sample connector by Wed 12/9 (in progress)
3) Stone: Nina to share evaluation set (prompts + expected labels) — ETA today
4) Redwood: Marcus to send baseline cost estimates after week 1 run

If you prefer a pre-kickoff call to walk the team through the runbook, I can do a 30-min slot Mon 12/7 10:30 PT. Otherwise see you on Tue.

Best,
Ava
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
